1	"""Typed models for Lindenmayer's eight custom kinds.
2	
3	Wire shapes follow docs/research/relay-integration/event-kinds.md §2
4	exactly; the portable per-kind specs live in docs/kinds/. Privacy is a
5	wire-format property here: run accounting is one rolled-up event per run
6	(no per-step model exists), and nothing in this API takes a per-worker
7	identity — ephemeral workers author no events.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import re
13	from datetime import datetime
14	from typing import Any, Literal
15	
16	from pydantic import Field, field_validator
17	
18	from lindenmayer.core.event import Event
19	from lindenmayer.core.kinds import constants
20	from lindenmayer.core.kinds.base import KindModel, KindValidationError
21	
22	__all__ = [
23	    "NodeLifecycle",
24	    "NodeStatePointer",
25	    "RunAccounting",
26	    "SubgraphDigest",
27	    "ApprovalRequest",
28	    "ApprovalVerdict",
29	    "TemplateVersion",
30	    "TemplatePointer",
31	]
32	
33	_HEX64 = re.compile(r"^[0-9a-f]{64}$")
34	
35	
36	def _check_hex64(value: str, what: str) -> str:
37	    if not _HEX64.fullmatch(value):
38	        raise ValueError(f"{what} must be 64-char lowercase hex, got {value!r}")
39	    return value
40	
41	
42	def _check_iso8601(value: str, what: str) -> str:
43	    try:
44	        datetime.fromisoformat(value)
45	    except ValueError as exc:
46	        raise ValueError(f"{what} must be an ISO 8601 timestamp: {exc}") from exc
47	    return value
48	
49	
50	class NodeLifecycle(KindModel):
51	    """Kind 42010 — one append-only event per node status transition."""
52	
53	    KIND = constants.KIND_NODE_LIFECYCLE
54	
55	    branch: str = Field(min_length=1)
56	    status: str = Field(min_length=1)
57	    run: str = Field(min_length=1)
58	    reason: str = ""
59	    parent_pubkey: str | None = None
60	    prev_event_id: str | None = None
61	
62	    @field_validator("parent_pubkey")
63	    @classmethod
64	    def _v_parent(cls, v: str | None) -> str | None:
65	        return None if v is None else _check_hex64(v, "parent_pubkey")
66	
67	    @field_validator("prev_event_id")
68	    @classmethod
69	    def _v_prev(cls, v: str | None) -> str | None:
70	        return None if v is None else _check_hex64(v, "prev_event_id")
71	
72	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
73	        tags = [
74	            ["branch", self.branch],
75	            ["status", self.status],
76	            ["run", self.run],
77	        ]
78	        if self.parent_pubkey is not None:
79	            tags.append(["p", self.parent_pubkey])
80	        if self.prev_event_id is not None:
81	            tags.append(["e", self.prev_event_id])
82	        return tags
83	
84	    def event_content(self) -> str:
85	        return self._dump_content({"reason": self.reason})
86	
87	    @classmethod
88	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
89	        content = cls._content_object(event)
90	        return {
91	            "branch": cls._require_tag(event, "branch"),
92	            "status": cls._require_tag(event, "status"),
93	            "run": cls._require_tag(event, "run"),
94	            "parent_pubkey": event.first_tag_value("p"),
95	            "prev_event_id": event.first_tag_value("e"),
96	            "reason": content.get("reason", ""),
97	        }
98	
99	
100	class NodeStatePointer(KindModel):
101	    """Kind 38110 — addressable latest-state snapshot, ``d`` = branch name."""
102	
103	    KIND = constants.KIND_NODE_STATE_POINTER
104	
105	    branch: str = Field(min_length=1)  # the addressable `d` key
106	    status: str = Field(min_length=1)
107	    run: str = Field(min_length=1)
108	    iter: str = Field(min_length=1)
109	    cost_shadow_usd: float = Field(ge=0)
110	    cost_cap_usd: float = Field(ge=0)
111	    last_lifecycle_event: str
112	
113	    @field_validator("last_lifecycle_event")
114	    @classmethod
115	    def _v_last(cls, v: str) -> str:
116	        return _check_hex64(v, "last_lifecycle_event")
117	
118	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
119	        return [
120	            ["d", self.branch],
121	            ["status", self.status],
122	            ["run", self.run],
123	            ["iter", self.iter],
124	        ]
125	
126	    def event_content(self) -> str:
127	        return self._dump_content(
128	            {
129	                "cost_shadow_usd": self.cost_shadow_usd,
130	                "cost_cap_usd": self.cost_cap_usd,
131	                "last_lifecycle_event": self.last_lifecycle_event,
132	            }
133	        )
134	
135	    @classmethod
136	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
137	        content = cls._content_object(event)
138	        return {
139	            "branch": cls._require_tag(event, "d"),
140	            "status": cls._require_tag(event, "status"),
141	            "run": cls._require_tag(event, "run"),
142	            "iter": cls._require_tag(event, "iter"),
143	            "cost_shadow_usd": content.get("cost_shadow_usd"),
144	            "cost_cap_usd": content.get("cost_cap_usd"),
145	            "last_lifecycle_event": content.get("last_lifecycle_event"),
146	        }
147	
148	
149	class RunAccounting(KindModel):
150	    """Kind 42020 — one rolled-up accounting event per completed run.
151	
152	    Deliberately run-grained: no per-step fields exist anywhere in this
153	    API (aggregates flow up, details stay in the subgraph). Costs are
154	    shadow cost, never real spend.
155	    """
156	
157	    KIND = constants.KIND_RUN_ACCOUNTING
158	
159	    branch: str = Field(min_length=1)
160	    run: str = Field(min_length=1)
161	    iter_count: int = Field(ge=0)
162	    cost_shadow_usd: float = Field(ge=0)
163	    duration_s: float = Field(ge=0)
164	    exit_status: Literal["completed", "exited", "killed"]
165	    template: str | None = None  # template version event id, when template-spawned
166	
167	    @field_validator("template")
168	    @classmethod
169	    def _v_template(cls, v: str | None) -> str | None:
170	        return None if v is None else _check_hex64(v, "template")
171	
172	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
173	        tags = [["branch", self.branch], ["run", self.run]]
174	        if self.template is not None:
175	            tags.append(["template", self.template])
176	        return tags
177	
178	    def event_content(self) -> str:
179	        return self._dump_content(
180	            {
181	                "iter_count": self.iter_count,
182	                "cost_shadow_usd": self.cost_shadow_usd,
183	                "duration_s": self.duration_s,
184	                "exit_status": self.exit_status,
185	            }
186	        )
187	
188	    @classmethod
189	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
190	        content = cls._content_object(event)
191	        return {
192	            "branch": cls._require_tag(event, "branch"),
193	            "run": cls._require_tag(event, "run"),
194	            "template": event.first_tag_value("template"),
195	            "iter_count": content.get("iter_count"),
196	            "cost_shadow_usd": content.get("cost_shadow_usd"),
197	            "duration_s": content.get("duration_s"),
198	            "exit_status": content.get("exit_status"),
199	        }
200	
201	
202	class SubgraphDigest(KindModel):
203	    """Kind 42030 — periodic aggregate a persistent node publishes about
204	    its own subtree. No per-child breakdown, by design."""
205	
206	    KIND = constants.KIND_SUBGRAPH_DIGEST
207	
208	    branch: str = Field(min_length=1)
209	    period_start: str
210	    period_end: str
211	    child_count: int = Field(ge=0)
212	    active: int = Field(ge=0)
213	    exited: int = Field(ge=0)
214	    completed: int = Field(ge=0)
215	    stuck_flagged: int = Field(ge=0)
216	    subtree_cost_shadow_usd: float = Field(ge=0)
217	
218	    @field_validator("period_start")
219	    @classmethod
220	    def _v_start(cls, v: str) -> str:
221	        return _check_iso8601(v, "period_start")
222	
223	    @field_validator("period_end")
224	    @classmethod
225	    def _v_end(cls, v: str) -> str:
226	        return _check_iso8601(v, "period_end")
227	
228	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
229	        return [
230	            ["branch", self.branch],
231	            ["period_start", self.period_start],
232	            ["period_end", self.period_end],
233	        ]
234	
235	    def event_content(self) -> str:
236	        return self._dump_content(
237	            {
238	                "child_count": self.child_count,
239	                "active": self.active,
240	                "exited": self.exited,
241	                "completed": self.completed,
242	                "stuck_flagged": self.stuck_flagged,
243	                "subtree_cost_shadow_usd": self.subtree_cost_shadow_usd,
244	            }
245	        )
246	
247	    @classmethod
248	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
249	        content = cls._content_object(event)
250	        return {
251	            "branch": cls._require_tag(event, "branch"),
252	            "period_start": cls._require_tag(event, "period_start"),
253	            "period_end": cls._require_tag(event, "period_end"),
254	            "child_count": content.get("child_count"),
255	            "active": content.get("active"),
256	            "exited": content.get("exited"),
257	            "completed": content.get("completed"),
258	            "stuck_flagged": content.get("stuck_flagged"),
259	            "subtree_cost_shadow_usd": content.get("subtree_cost_shadow_usd"),
260	        }
261	
262	
263	class ApprovalRequest(KindModel):
264	    """Kind 42040 — a ``requires_approval`` step asking its approver."""
265	
266	    KIND = constants.KIND_APPROVAL_REQUEST
267	
268	    branch: str = Field(min_length=1)
269	    run: str = Field(min_length=1)
270	    iter: str = Field(min_length=1)
271	    step: str = Field(min_length=1)
272	    approver_pubkey: str
273	    step_name: str = Field(min_length=1)
274	    summary: str
275	
276	    @field_validator("approver_pubkey")
277	    @classmethod
278	    def _v_approver(cls, v: str) -> str:
279	        return _check_hex64(v, "approver_pubkey")
280	
281	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
282	        return [
283	            ["branch", self.branch],
284	            ["run", self.run],
285	            ["iter", self.iter],
286	            ["step", self.step],
287	            ["p", self.approver_pubkey],
288	        ]
289	
290	    def event_content(self) -> str:
291	        return self._dump_content({"step_name": self.step_name, "summary": self.summary})
292	
293	    @classmethod
294	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
295	        content = cls._content_object(event)
296	        return {
297	            "branch": cls._require_tag(event, "branch"),
298	            "run": cls._require_tag(event, "run"),
299	            "iter": cls._require_tag(event, "iter"),
300	            "step": cls._require_tag(event, "step"),
301	            "approver_pubkey": cls._require_tag(event, "p"),
302	            "step_name": content.get("step_name"),
303	            "summary": content.get("summary"),
304	        }
305	
306	
307	class ApprovalVerdict(KindModel):
308	    """Kind 42041 — answers a request by ``e``-tagging it; append-only so
309	    reject→revise→approve chains stay threaded."""
310	
311	    KIND = constants.KIND_APPROVAL_VERDICT
312	
313	    request_id: str
314	    verdict: Literal["approve", "reject"]
315	    rationale: str
316	
317	    @field_validator("request_id")
318	    @classmethod
319	    def _v_request(cls, v: str) -> str:
320	        return _check_hex64(v, "request_id")
321	
322	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
323	        return [["e", self.request_id], ["verdict", self.verdict]]
324	
325	    def event_content(self) -> str:
326	        return self._dump_content({"rationale": self.rationale})
327	
328	    @classmethod
329	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
330	        content = cls._content_object(event)
331	        return {
332	            "request_id": cls._require_tag(event, "e"),
333	            "verdict": cls._require_tag(event, "verdict"),
334	            "rationale": content.get("rationale"),
335	        }
336	
337	
338	class TemplateVersion(KindModel):
339	    """Kind 42050 — one immutable event per registered template version."""
340	
341	    KIND = constants.KIND_TEMPLATE_VERSION
342	
343	    template_name: str = Field(min_length=1)
344	    version: str = Field(min_length=1)
345	    summary: str
346	    inherits: str | None = None  # parent template version event id
347	    git_ref: str | None = None
348	
349	    @field_validator("inherits")
350	    @classmethod
351	    def _v_inherits(cls, v: str | None) -> str | None:
352	        return None if v is None else _check_hex64(v, "inherits")
353	
354	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
355	        tags = [["template_name", self.template_name], ["version", self.version]]
356	        if self.inherits is not None:
357	            # NIP-10 style marked e-tag: ["e", <id>, <relay-url>, <marker>]
358	            tags.append(["e", self.inherits, "", "inherit"])
359	        if self.git_ref is not None:
360	            tags.append(["git_ref", self.git_ref])
361	        return tags
362	
363	    def event_content(self) -> str:
364	        return self._dump_content({"summary": self.summary})
365	
366	    @classmethod
367	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
368	        content = cls._content_object(event)
369	        inherits = None
370	        for values in event.tag_values("e"):
371	            if len(values) >= 3 and values[2] == "inherit":
372	                inherits = values[0]
373	                break
374	        return {
375	            "template_name": cls._require_tag(event, "template_name"),
376	            "version": cls._require_tag(event, "version"),
377	            "inherits": inherits,
378	            "git_ref": event.first_tag_value("git_ref"),
379	            "summary": content.get("summary"),
380	        }
381	
382	
383	class TemplatePointer(KindModel):
384	    """Kind 38150 — addressable pointer to the current template version,
385	    ``d`` = template name. Pure indirection: content is empty."""
386	
387	    KIND = constants.KIND_TEMPLATE_POINTER
388	
389	    template_name: str = Field(min_length=1)  # the addressable `d` key
390	    version_event_id: str
391	
392	    @field_validator("version_event_id")
393	    @classmethod
394	    def _v_version(cls, v: str) -> str:
395	        return _check_hex64(v, "version_event_id")
396	
397	    def event_tags(self) -> list[list[str]]:
398	        return [["d", self.template_name], ["e", self.version_event_id]]
399	
400	    def event_content(self) -> str:
401	        return ""
402	
403	    @classmethod
404	    def _from_wire(cls, event: Event) -> dict[str, Any]:
405	        # Spec: content is empty or "{}" — anything else is malformed.
406	        if event.content not in ("", "{}"):
407	            raise KindValidationError(
408	                f"kind {cls.KIND} (TemplatePointer) content must be empty or '{{}}', "
409	                f"got {event.content!r}"
410	            )
411	        return {
412	            "template_name": cls._require_tag(event, "d"),
413	            "version_event_id": cls._require_tag(event, "e"),
414	        }
415

1	"""NIP-01 event model — the trust root of the platform.
2	
3	Canonical id computation, signing, and verification for Nostr events.
4	Every other Lindenmayer component consumes events through this module;
5	nothing here knows about Lindenmayer kinds, relays, or Buzz.
6	
7	Canonical serialization follows NIP-01: the id is the SHA-256 of the
8	UTF-8 JSON array ``[0, pubkey, created_at, kind, tags, content]`` with no
9	whitespace. Python's ``json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False, separators=(",", ":"))``
10	produces the same escaping as ``JSON.stringify`` (the de-facto reference
11	via nostr-tools), which is what published event ids are computed with.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import hashlib
17	import json
18	import time
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from typing import Any, Iterator
21	
22	import lindenmayer.core.keys as _keys
23	
24	__all__ = ["Event", "EventValidationError", "canonical_serialization", "compute_event_id"]
25	
26	
27	class EventValidationError(ValueError):
28	    """Raised when an event fails structural validation."""
29	
30	
31	def canonical_serialization(
32	    pubkey: str, created_at: int, kind: int, tags: list[list[str]], content: str
33	) -> bytes:
34	    """The NIP-01 canonical byte serialization whose SHA-256 is the event id."""
35	    return json.dumps(
36	        [0, pubkey, created_at, kind, tags, content],
37	        ensure_ascii=False,
38	        separators=(",", ":"),
39	    ).encode("utf-8")
40	
41	
42	def compute_event_id(
43	    pubkey: str, created_at: int, kind: int, tags: list[list[str]], content: str
44	) -> str:
45	    """Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the canonical serialization."""
46	    return hashlib.sha256(
47	        canonical_serialization(pubkey, created_at, kind, tags, content)
48	    ).hexdigest()
49	
50	
51	def _is_hex(value: str, length: int) -> bool:
52	    if len(value) != length or value != value.lower():
53	        return False
54	    try:
55	        bytes.fromhex(value)
56	    except ValueError:
57	        return False
58	    return True
59	
60	
61	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
62	class Event:
63	    """An immutable NIP-01 event.
64	
65	    ``tags`` is stored as a tuple of tuples so instances are hashable; the
66	    wire form (``to_dict``/``to_json``) uses lists per NIP-01.
67	    """
68	
69	    pubkey: str
70	    created_at: int
71	    kind: int
72	    tags: tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...]
73	    content: str
74	    id: str
75	    sig: str | None = field(default=None)
76	
77	    # -- construction ------------------------------------------------------
78	
79	    @classmethod
80	    def build(
81	        cls,
82	        *,
83	        pubkey: str,
84	        kind: int,
85	        tags: list[list[str]] | tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...] = (),
86	        content: str = "",
87	        created_at: int | None = None,
88	    ) -> "Event":
89	        """Build an unsigned event with a freshly computed id."""
90	        ts = int(time.time()) if created_at is None else int(created_at)
91	        tag_lists = [list(t) for t in tags]
92	        event_id = compute_event_id(pubkey, ts, kind, tag_lists, content)
93	        return cls(
94	            pubkey=pubkey,
95	            created_at=ts,
96	            kind=kind,
97	            tags=tuple(tuple(t) for t in tags),
98	            content=content,
99	            id=event_id,
100	        )
101	
102	    @classmethod
103	    def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> "Event":
104	        """Parse a wire-format event dict, validating structure (not signature)."""
105	        try:
106	            pubkey = data["pubkey"]
107	            created_at = data["created_at"]
108	            kind = data["kind"]
109	            tags = data["tags"]
110	            content = data["content"]
111	            event_id = data["id"]
112	        except (KeyError, TypeError) as exc:
113	            raise EventValidationError(f"missing event field: {exc}") from exc
114	        sig = data.get("sig")
115	        if not isinstance(pubkey, str) or not _is_hex(pubkey, 64):
116	            raise EventValidationError("pubkey must be 64-char lowercase hex")
117	        if not isinstance(event_id, str) or not _is_hex(event_id, 64):
118	            raise EventValidationError("id must be 64-char lowercase hex")
119	        if sig is not None and (not isinstance(sig, str) or not _is_hex(sig, 128)):
120	            raise EventValidationError("sig must be 128-char lowercase hex")
121	        if not isinstance(created_at, int) or isinstance(created_at, bool):
122	            raise EventValidationError("created_at must be an integer")
123	        if not isinstance(kind, int) or isinstance(kind, bool) or not 0 <= kind <= 65535:
124	            raise EventValidationError("kind must be an integer in [0, 65535]")
125	        if not isinstance(content, str):
126	            raise EventValidationError("content must be a string")
127	        if not isinstance(tags, list) or any(
128	            not isinstance(t, list) or any(not isinstance(v, str) for v in t) for t in tags
129	        ):
130	            raise EventValidationError("tags must be a list of lists of strings")
131	        return cls(
132	            pubkey=pubkey,
133	            created_at=created_at,
134	            kind=kind,
135	            tags=tuple(tuple(t) for t in tags),
136	            content=content,
137	            id=event_id,
138	            sig=sig,
139	        )
140	
141	    @classmethod
142	    def from_json(cls, raw: str | bytes) -> "Event":
143	        try:
144	            data = json.loads(raw)
145	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
146	            raise EventValidationError(f"invalid JSON: {exc}") from exc
147	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
148	            raise EventValidationError("event JSON must be an object")
149	        return cls.from_dict(data)
150	
151	    # -- serialization -----------------------------------------------------
152	
153	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
154	        data: dict[str, Any] = {
155	            "id": self.id,
156	            "pubkey": self.pubkey,
157	            "created_at": self.created_at,
158	            "kind": self.kind,
159	            "tags": [list(t) for t in self.tags],
160	            "content": self.content,
161	        }
162	        if self.sig is not None:
163	            data["sig"] = self.sig
164	        return data
165	
166	    def to_json(self) -> str:
167	        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), ensure_ascii=False, separators=(",", ":"))
168	
169	    # -- integrity ---------------------------------------------------------
170	
171	    def recompute_id(self) -> str:
172	        return compute_event_id(
173	            self.pubkey, self.created_at, self.kind, [list(t) for t in self.tags], self.content
174	        )
175	
176	    def id_valid(self) -> bool:
177	        """Does the declared id match the canonical serialization?"""
178	        return self.id == self.recompute_id()
179	
180	    def sig_valid(self) -> bool:
181	        """Is the signature a valid BIP-340 signature by ``pubkey`` over ``id``?
182	
183	        False when unsigned. Does not check the id itself — use ``verify``.
184	        """
185	        if self.sig is None:
186	            return False
187	        return _keys.schnorr_verify(self.pubkey, bytes.fromhex(self.id), bytes.fromhex(self.sig))
188	
189	    def verify(self) -> bool:
190	        """Full NIP-01 verification: id matches content AND signature is valid.
191	
192	        This is the platform's trust root — every consumer that reads from a
193	        relay must call this (or a helper that does) before believing an event.
194	        """
195	        return self.id_valid() and self.sig_valid()
196	
197	    # -- tag access --------------------------------------------------------
198	
199	    def tag_values(self, name: str) -> Iterator[tuple[str, ...]]:
200	        """Yield the value portions (everything after the name) of matching tags."""
201	        for tag in self.tags:
202	            if tag and tag[0] == name:
203	                yield tag[1:]
204	
205	    def first_tag_value(self, name: str) -> str | None:
206	        """The first value of the first tag named ``name``, or None."""
207	        for values in self.tag_values(name):
208	            return values[0] if values else None
209	        return None
210

1	"""Read template versions from a relay.
2	
3	Query a relay for 42050/38150 events by author and template name, reconstruct
4	the full version history, and verify signatures and author attestation via
5	core's verification module. Version history is ordered by the `version` tag
6	(treating `created_at` as informational — git commit timestamps are not
7	guaranteed monotonic per condition 2, registry countersign).
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	
14	from lindenmayer.core.event import Event
15	from lindenmayer.core.verify import AttestationOutcome, validate_attestation
16	
17	__all__ = ["TemplateReader", "TemplateVersion", "ReaderError"]
18	
19	
20	class ReaderError(Exception):
21	    """Template reader operation failed."""
22	
23	    pass
24	
25	
26	@dataclass
27	class TemplateVersion:
28	    """A single template version from the relay."""
29	
30	    name: str
31	    version: str
32	    git_ref: str
33	    created_at: int
34	    summary: str
35	    event_id: str
36	    parent_version_id: str | None = None
37	
38	
39	class TemplateReader:
40	    """Read and validate template versions from a relay."""
41	
42	    def __init__(self, relay_client) -> None:
43	        """Initialize reader with a relay client.
44	
45	        Args:
46	            relay_client: Core RelayClient instance
47	        """
48	        self._relay = relay_client
49	
50	    async def get_version_history(self, author: str, template_name: str) -> list[TemplateVersion]:
51	        """Fetch and reconstruct full version history for a template.
52	
53	        Queries the relay for all kind 42050 events by author with matching
54	        template_name tag, verifies signatures and attestation, and returns
55	        them sorted by version (created_at is informational only).
56	
57	        Args:
58	            author: Pubkey of the template author
59	            template_name: Name of the template
60	
61	        Returns:
62	            List of TemplateVersion objects sorted by version (stable order)
63	
64	        Raises:
65	            ReaderError: If query fails or events fail verification
66	        """
67	        try:
68	            filters = [
69	                {
70	                    "kinds": [42050],
71	                    "authors": [author],
72	                    "#template_name": [template_name],
73	                }
74	            ]
75	            events = await self._relay.query(filters)
76	        except Exception as e:
77	            raise ReaderError(f"relay query failed: {e}") from e
78	
79	        versions: list[TemplateVersion] = []
80	        for event in events:
81	            # Verify event integrity
82	            if not event.verify():
83	                raise ReaderError(f"event {event.id} failed NIP-01 verification")
84	
85	            # Verify author attestation (if present)
86	            result = validate_attestation(event)
87	            if result.outcome == AttestationOutcome.INVALID:
88	                raise ReaderError(f"event {event.id} has invalid attestation: {result.reason}")
89	
90	            # Extract version metadata
91	            version_name = event.first_tag_value("template_name")
92	            version_str = event.first_tag_value("version")
93	            git_ref = event.first_tag_value("git_ref")
94	
95	            if not version_name or not version_str:
96	                raise ReaderError(f"event {event.id} missing template_name or version tag")
97	
98	            # Parse content
99	            import json
100	
101	            try:
102	                content = json.loads(event.content)
103	                summary = content.get("summary", "")
104	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
105	                summary = ""
106	
107	            # Check for parent version (inheritance)
108	            # Tag format: ["e", "<id>", "", "inherit"]
109	            # tag_values("e") yields ("<id>", "", "inherit")
110	            parent_id = None
111	            for tag_vals in event.tag_values("e"):
112	                if len(tag_vals) >= 3 and tag_vals[2] == "inherit":
113	                    parent_id = tag_vals[0]
114	                    break
115	
116	            versions.append(
117	                TemplateVersion(
118	                    name=version_name,
119	                    version=version_str,
120	                    git_ref=git_ref or "",
121	                    created_at=event.created_at,
122	                    summary=summary,
123	                    event_id=event.id,
124	                    parent_version_id=parent_id,
125	                )
126	            )
127	
128	        # Sort by version string (stable ordering, not wall-clock time)
129	        # Use natural version comparison if versions are semantic, else lexical
130	        versions.sort(key=lambda v: _version_sort_key(v.version))
131	        return versions
132	
133	    async def get_current_pointer(self, author: str, template_name: str) -> str | None:
134	        """Get the current template version from the addressable pointer.
135	
136	        Queries kind 38150 (Template Pointer) by author and d-tag for the
137	        template name, returns the current version event id if found.
138	
139	        Args:
140	            author: Pubkey of the template author
141	            template_name: Name of the template
142	
143	        Returns:
144	            Event id of the current version, or None if no pointer found
145	
146	        Raises:
147	            ReaderError: If query fails or pointer is invalid
148	        """
149	        try:
150	            filters = [
151	                {
152	                    "kinds": [38150],
153	                    "authors": [author],
154	                    "#d": [template_name],
155	                }
156	            ]
157	            events = await self._relay.query(filters)
158	        except Exception as e:
159	            raise ReaderError(f"relay query failed: {e}") from e
160	
161	        if not events:
162	            return None
163	
164	        # Most recent addressable event wins (relays collapse these)
165	        latest = max(events, key=lambda e: (e.created_at, e.id))
166	
167	        if not latest.verify():
168	            raise ReaderError(f"pointer event {latest.id} failed NIP-01 verification")
169	
170	        version_id = latest.first_tag_value("e")
171	        if not version_id:
172	            raise ReaderError(f"pointer event {latest.id} missing e tag")
173	
174	        return version_id
175	
176	
177	def _version_sort_key(version: str) -> tuple:
178	    """Convert semantic version string to a sortable tuple.
179	
180	    Handles both semantic versioning (1.2.3) and single numbers (1).
181	    Falls back to lexical sort on parse failure.
182	    """
183	    try:
184	        parts = version.split(".")
185	        return tuple(int(p) for p in parts)
186	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
187	        # Fallback to lexical sort if not semantic
188	        return (version,)

"""Read template versions from a relay.

Query a relay for 42050/38150 events by author and template name, reconstruct
the full version history, and verify signatures and author attestation via
core's verification module. Version history is ordered by the `version` tag
(treating `created_at` as informational — git commit timestamps are not
guaranteed monotonic per condition 2, registry countersign).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass

from lindenmayer.core.event import Event
from lindenmayer.core.verify import AttestationOutcome, validate_attestation

__all__ = ["TemplateReader", "TemplateVersion", "ReaderError"]


class ReaderError(Exception):
    """Template reader operation failed."""

    pass


@dataclass
class TemplateVersion:
    """A single template version from the relay."""

    name: str
    version: str
    git_ref: str
    created_at: int
    summary: str
    event_id: str
    parent_version_id: str | None = None


class TemplateReader:
    """Read and validate template versions from a relay."""

    def __init__(self, relay_client) -> None:
        """Initialize reader with a relay client.

        Args:
            relay_client: Core RelayClient instance
        """
        self._relay = relay_client

    async def get_version_history(self, author: str, template_name: str) -> list[TemplateVersion]:
        """Fetch and reconstruct full version history for a template.

        Queries the relay for all kind 42050 events by author with matching
        template_name tag, verifies signatures and attestation, and returns
        them sorted by version (created_at is informational only).

        Args:
            author: Pubkey of the template author
            template_name: Name of the template

        Returns:
            List of TemplateVersion objects sorted by version (stable order)

        Raises:
            ReaderError: If query fails or events fail verification
        """
        try:
            filters = [
                {
                    "kinds": [42050],
                    "authors": [author],
                    "#template_name": [template_name],
                }
            ]
            events = await self._relay.query(filters)
        except Exception as e:
            raise ReaderError(f"relay query failed: {e}") from e

        versions: list[TemplateVersion] = []
        for event in events:
            # Verify event integrity
            if not event.verify():
                raise ReaderError(f"event {event.id} failed NIP-01 verification")

            # Verify author attestation (if present)
            result = validate_attestation(event)
            if result.outcome == AttestationOutcome.INVALID:
                raise ReaderError(f"event {event.id} has invalid attestation: {result.reason}")

            # Extract version metadata
            version_name = event.first_tag_value("template_name")
            version_str = event.first_tag_value("version")
            git_ref = event.first_tag_value("git_ref")

            if not version_name or not version_str:
                raise ReaderError(f"event {event.id} missing template_name or version tag")

            # Parse content
            import json

            try:
                content = json.loads(event.content)
                summary = content.get("summary", "")
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
                summary = ""

            # Check for parent version (inheritance)
            # Tag format: ["e", "<id>", "", "inherit"]
            # tag_values("e") yields ("<id>", "", "inherit")
            parent_id = None
            for tag_vals in event.tag_values("e"):
                if len(tag_vals) >= 3 and tag_vals[2] == "inherit":
                    parent_id = tag_vals[0]
                    break

            versions.append(
                TemplateVersion(
                    name=version_name,
                    version=version_str,
                    git_ref=git_ref or "",
                    created_at=event.created_at,
                    summary=summary,
                    event_id=event.id,
                    parent_version_id=parent_id,
                )
            )

        # Sort by version string (stable ordering, not wall-clock time)
        # Use natural version comparison if versions are semantic, else lexical
        versions.sort(key=lambda v: _version_sort_key(v.version))
        return versions

    async def get_current_pointer(self, author: str, template_name: str) -> str | None:
        """Get the current template version from the addressable pointer.

        Queries kind 38150 (Template Pointer) by author and d-tag for the
        template name, returns the current version event id if found.

        Args:
            author: Pubkey of the template author
            template_name: Name of the template

        Returns:
            Event id of the current version, or None if no pointer found

        Raises:
            ReaderError: If query fails or pointer is invalid
        """
        try:
            filters = [
                {
                    "kinds": [38150],
                    "authors": [author],
                    "#d": [template_name],
                }
            ]
            events = await self._relay.query(filters)
        except Exception as e:
            raise ReaderError(f"relay query failed: {e}") from e

        if not events:
            return None

        # Most recent addressable event wins (relays collapse these)
        latest = max(events, key=lambda e: (e.created_at, e.id))

        if not latest.verify():
            raise ReaderError(f"pointer event {latest.id} failed NIP-01 verification")

        version_id = latest.first_tag_value("e")
        if not version_id:
            raise ReaderError(f"pointer event {latest.id} missing e tag")

        return version_id


def _version_sort_key(version: str) -> tuple:
    """Convert semantic version string to a sortable tuple.

    Handles both semantic versioning (1.2.3) and single numbers (1).
    Falls back to lexical sort on parse failure.
    """
    try:
        parts = version.split(".")
        return tuple(int(p) for p in parts)
    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
        # Fallback to lexical sort if not semantic
        return (version,)

"""Validate instance → template-version associations.

Parse instance contracts' template linkage lines (`template: <name> v<N> @ <sha>`),
validate that the pin resolves and matches a registered version, and expose the
instance → template-version association.

READ-SIDE ONLY, terminating at the 42050 version event id: no new event kinds,
no wire-visible association artifacts, no eval-shaped schema.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = ["TemplateLinkage", "LinkageValidator", "LinkageError"]


class LinkageError(Exception):
    """Template linkage validation failed."""

    pass


@dataclass
class TemplateLinkage:
    """A parsed template linkage line from an instance contract."""

    name: str
    version: str
    sha: str
    instance_path: Path | None = None


def parse_template_linkage_line(line: str) -> TemplateLinkage:
    """Parse a template linkage line from NODE.md.

    Expected format: `template: <name> v<N> @ <sha>`
    Handles variations with backticks, markdown, extra whitespace, etc.

    Args:
        line: Raw line from NODE.md

    Returns:
        Parsed TemplateLinkage

    Raises:
        LinkageError: If line format is invalid
    """
    # Match: template: dev-node v1 @ 9f147a3
    # Also handles:
    #   **template:** `default-node v2.1.0 @ abc1234567`
    #   template:  test v1 @ abc1234
    # After template:, skip spaces, *, and `, then capture name (alphanumeric+dash)
    pattern = r"template:[\s*`]*([a-z0-9-]+)\s+v([\d\.]+)\s+@\s+([a-f0-9]+)"
    match = re.search(pattern, line, re.IGNORECASE)
    if not match:
        raise LinkageError(f"invalid template linkage line: {line}")
    name, version, sha = match.groups()
    return TemplateLinkage(name=name, version=version, sha=sha)


class LinkageValidator:
    """Validate instance contracts against registered template versions."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        """Initialize validator."""
        pass

    def extract_from_node_md(self, node_md_path: Path) -> TemplateLinkage:
        """Extract template linkage from an instance NODE.md file.

        Searches the file for a line matching `template: <name> v<N> @ <sha>`.

        Args:
            node_md_path: Path to NODE.md file

        Returns:
            Parsed TemplateLinkage

        Raises:
            LinkageError: If linkage line not found or invalid
        """
        try:
            content = node_md_path.read_text()
        except Exception as e:
            raise LinkageError(f"cannot read {node_md_path}: {e}") from e

        # Real contracts may mention "template:" in prose (e.g. when quoting
        # the linkage format itself); skip lines that don't parse and take
        # the first that does.
        for line in content.split("\n"):
            if "template:" in line:
                try:
                    linkage = parse_template_linkage_line(line)
                except LinkageError:
                    continue
                linkage.instance_path = node_md_path
                return linkage

        raise LinkageError(f"no template linkage line found in {node_md_path}")

    def validate_against_version(
        self, linkage: TemplateLinkage, version_event_id: str, git_ref: str
    ) -> bool:
        """Validate that a linkage references a known version event.

        Checks that:
        1. The git_ref from the version event matches (or starts with) the
           sha in the linkage line
        2. The version event id can be used as the anchor for future evals

        Args:
            linkage: Parsed template linkage
            version_event_id: The kind 42050 event id for this version
            git_ref: The git_ref tag value from the version event

        Returns:
            True if linkage is valid

        Raises:
            LinkageError: If validation fails
        """
        # Check that git_ref from version event matches linkage sha
        # Allow short shas (e.g., 9f147a3) matching the beginning of full shas
        if not git_ref.startswith(linkage.sha) and not linkage.sha.startswith(git_ref):
            raise LinkageError(
                f"linkage sha {linkage.sha} does not match version event git_ref {git_ref}"
            )
        return True

    def resolve_linkage(
        self, linkage: TemplateLinkage, versions: list[dict]
    ) -> str | None:
        """Resolve a linkage to a version event id.

        Searches the given version list for a matching name and version.

        Args:
            linkage: Parsed template linkage
            versions: List of template versions (dicts with name, version, event_id keys)

        Returns:
            Event id of the matching version, or None if not found

        Raises:
            LinkageError: If multiple versions match (ambiguous)
        """
        matches = [
            v for v in versions if v.get("name") == linkage.name and v.get("version") == linkage.version
        ]
        if not matches:
            return None
        if len(matches) > 1:
            raise LinkageError(
                f"ambiguous template version: {linkage.name} v{linkage.version} "
                f"matches {len(matches)} events"
            )
        return matches[0].get("event_id")

"""An in-process NIP-01/29/42 mock relay for exercising RelayClient.

Owned by the relay leaf alongside tests/test_relay.py. Speaks just enough of
the wire protocol to drive the tests: EVENT -> OK, REQ -> stored EVENTs ->
EOSE (plus live fan-out on later publishes), CLOSE, and an optional
auth-required mode that challenges on connect and rejects EVENT/REQ with
``auth-required:``-prefixed OK/CLOSED messages until a valid signed kind-22242
AUTH event answers the challenge.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import secrets
from typing import Any

import websockets

from lindenmayer.core.event import Event, EventValidationError


def _matches(event_data: dict[str, Any], filt: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    if "ids" in filt and event_data["id"] not in filt["ids"]:
        return False
    if "authors" in filt and event_data["pubkey"] not in filt["authors"]:
        return False
    if "kinds" in filt and event_data["kind"] not in filt["kinds"]:
        return False
    for key, values in filt.items():
        if key.startswith("#") and len(key) == 2:
            tag_name = key[1]
            tag_values = {
                t[1] for t in event_data.get("tags", []) if len(t) > 1 and t[0] == tag_name
            }
            if not tag_values & set(values):
                return False
    return True


def _matches_any(event_data: dict[str, Any], filters: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool:
    return any(_matches(event_data, f) for f in filters)


class MockRelay:
    """A minimal in-process relay server for tests. One instance per test."""

    def __init__(self, *, require_auth: bool = False, reject_kinds: set[int] | None = None) -> None:
        self.require_auth = require_auth
        self.reject_kinds = reject_kinds or set()
        self.events: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.url: str | None = None
        self._server: Any = None
        self._subs: dict[Any, dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]] = {}
        self._challenges: dict[Any, str] = {}
        self._authed: set[Any] = set()

    async def start(self) -> "MockRelay":
        self._server = await websockets.serve(self._handler, "localhost", 0)
        port = self._server.sockets[0].getsockname()[1]
        self.url = f"ws://localhost:{port}"
        return self

    async def stop(self) -> None:
        self._server.close()
        await self._server.wait_closed()

    async def __aenter__(self) -> "MockRelay":
        return await self.start()

    async def __aexit__(self, *exc_info: object) -> None:
        await self.stop()

    # -- connection handling ------------------------------------------------

    async def _handler(self, ws: Any) -> None:
        self._subs[ws] = {}
        if self.require_auth:
            challenge = secrets.token_hex(16)
            self._challenges[ws] = challenge
            await ws.send(json.dumps(["AUTH", challenge]))
        try:
            async for raw in ws:
                await self._dispatch(ws, json.loads(raw))
        finally:
            self._subs.pop(ws, None)
            self._challenges.pop(ws, None)
            self._authed.discard(ws)

    async def _dispatch(self, ws: Any, msg: list[Any]) -> None:
        msg_type = msg[0]
        if msg_type == "EVENT":
            await self._handle_event(ws, msg[1])
        elif msg_type == "REQ":
            await self._handle_req(ws, msg[1], msg[2:])
        elif msg_type == "CLOSE":
            self._subs[ws].pop(msg[1], None)
        elif msg_type == "AUTH":
            await self._handle_auth(ws, msg[1])

    # -- protocol handlers ----------------------------------------------------

    async def _handle_event(self, ws: Any, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        event_id = event_data.get("id", "")
        if self.require_auth and ws not in self._authed:
            await ws.send(json.dumps(["OK", event_id, False, "auth-required: please authenticate"]))
            return
        if event_data.get("kind") in self.reject_kinds:
            await ws.send(json.dumps(["OK", event_id, False, "blocked: kind not allowed"]))
            return
        self.events.append(event_data)
        await ws.send(json.dumps(["OK", event_id, True, ""]))
        for conn, subs in self._subs.items():
            for sub_id, filters in subs.items():
                if _matches_any(event_data, filters):
                    await conn.send(json.dumps(["EVENT", sub_id, event_data]))

    async def _handle_req(self, ws: Any, sub_id: str, filters: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
        if self.require_auth and ws not in self._authed:
            await ws.send(json.dumps(["CLOSED", sub_id, "auth-required: please authenticate"]))
            return
        self._subs[ws][sub_id] = filters
        for event_data in self.events:
            if _matches_any(event_data, filters):
                await ws.send(json.dumps(["EVENT", sub_id, event_data]))
        await ws.send(json.dumps(["EOSE", sub_id]))

    async def _handle_auth(self, ws: Any, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        try:
            event = Event.from_dict(event_data)
        except EventValidationError:
            await ws.send(json.dumps(["OK", event_data.get("id", ""), False, "malformed auth event"]))
            return
        challenge = self._challenges.get(ws)
        ok = (
            event.verify()
            and event.kind == 22242
            and event.first_tag_value("challenge") == challenge
        )
        if ok:
            self._authed.add(ws)
        await ws.send(json.dumps(["OK", event.id, ok, "" if ok else "invalid auth event"]))

"""End-to-end dogfood test: register dev-node v1 and read back."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from lindenmayer.core.config import CoreConfig
from lindenmayer.core.keys import Keypair
from lindenmayer.core.relay import RelayClient
from lindenmayer.registry.publisher import TemplatePublisher
from lindenmayer.registry.reader import TemplateReader
from relay_mock import MockRelay


@pytest.fixture
def repo_root() -> Path:
    """Get the repo root."""
    return Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent


@pytest.fixture
def fixture_template_dir() -> Path:
    """Get the fixture template directory (copy of dev-node)."""
    return Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures" / "registry" / "dev-node"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_e2e_dogfood_register_and_read(
    repo_root: Path, fixture_template_dir: Path
) -> None:
    """End-to-end: register dev-node v1 and read back history."""
    keypair = Keypair.generate()

    # Create mock relay
    async with MockRelay() as relay:
        config = CoreConfig(relay_url=relay.url)
        # Create publisher
        publisher = TemplatePublisher(repo_root, keypair)

        # Extract template info from fixture
        template_info = publisher.extract_template_info(fixture_template_dir)
        assert template_info.name == "dev-node"
        assert template_info.version == "1"

        # Build and sign events
        version_event = publisher.build_version_event(template_info)
        signed_version = keypair.sign_event(version_event)

        pointer_event = publisher.build_pointer_event(version_event)
        signed_pointer = keypair.sign_event(pointer_event)

        # Publish to mock relay (bypass client)
        relay.events.append(signed_version.to_dict())
        relay.events.append(signed_pointer.to_dict())

        # Create reader and read back
        async with RelayClient(relay.url, keypair, config) as client:
            reader = TemplateReader(client)

            # Read version history
            versions = await reader.get_version_history(keypair.public_key_hex, "dev-node")

            assert len(versions) == 1
            version = versions[0]
            assert version.name == "dev-node"
            assert version.version == "1"
            assert version.event_id == signed_version.id

            # Read current pointer
            current_id = await reader.get_current_pointer(keypair.public_key_hex, "dev-node")
            assert current_id == signed_version.id


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_e2e_republish_idempotency(fixture_template_dir: Path) -> None:
    """Republishing the same template produces identical event ids."""
    keypair1 = Keypair.generate()

    repo_root = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent

    # Publish with keypair1
    async with MockRelay() as relay:
        publisher1 = TemplatePublisher(repo_root, keypair1)
        info1 = publisher1.extract_template_info(fixture_template_dir)
        version1 = publisher1.build_version_event(info1)
        signed1 = keypair1.sign_event(version1)

        relay.events.append(signed1.to_dict())

        # Publish again with same keypair
        version1_again = publisher1.build_version_event(info1)
        signed1_again = keypair1.sign_event(version1_again)

        # Event ids must be identical
        assert signed1.id == signed1_again.id


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_e2e_multiple_versions(repo_root: Path, fixture_template_dir: Path) -> None:
    """Test reading multiple versions of a template."""
    import json

    keypair = Keypair.generate()

    async with MockRelay() as relay:
        config = CoreConfig(relay_url=relay.url)
        from lindenmayer.core.event import Event

        # Create version 1 event manually
        v1_event = Event.build(
            pubkey=keypair.public_key_hex,
            kind=42050,
            tags=[
                ["template_name", "multi-test"],
                ["version", "1.0.0"],
                ["git_ref", "abc123"],
            ],
            content=json.dumps({"summary": "Initial release"}),
            created_at=1000000000,
        )
        signed_v1 = keypair.sign_event(v1_event)

        # Create version 2 event
        v2_event = Event.build(
            pubkey=keypair.public_key_hex,
            kind=42050,
            tags=[
                ["template_name", "multi-test"],
                ["version", "2.0.0"],
                ["git_ref", "def456"],
            ],
            content=json.dumps({"summary": "Add features"}),
            created_at=1000001000,
        )
        signed_v2 = keypair.sign_event(v2_event)

        # Publish both
        relay.events.append(signed_v1.to_dict())
        relay.events.append(signed_v2.to_dict())

        # Read history
        async with RelayClient(relay.url, keypair, config) as client:
            reader = TemplateReader(client)
            versions = await reader.get_version_history(keypair.public_key_hex, "multi-test")

            assert len(versions) == 2
            # Should be sorted by version, not created_at
            assert versions[0].version == "1.0.0"
            assert versions[1].version == "2.0.0"
            assert versions[0].summary == "Initial release"
            assert versions[1].summary == "Add features"

"""Pytest configuration for bridge tests.

Sets up paths to allow imports from both the main repo and the worktree.
"""

import sys
import os

# Add main repo src to path
main_repo_src = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", "..", "..", "src")
if main_repo_src not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, main_repo_src)

# Add worktree src to path
worktree_src = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", "src")
if worktree_src not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, worktree_src)

"""Command-line interface for template registry operations.

Commands:
  publish <template_dir> - Publish a template version to the relay
  list <author>          - List all templates from an author
  show <author> <name>   - Show version history for a template
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import asyncio
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from lindenmayer.core.config import CoreConfig
from lindenmayer.core.keys import Keypair
from lindenmayer.core.relay import RelayClient
from lindenmayer.registry.publisher import TemplatePublisher
from lindenmayer.registry.reader import TemplateReader

__all__ = ["main"]


def _create_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    """Create the argument parser."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="lindenmayer-registry",
        description="Lindenmayer template registry: publish and query template versions",
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--relay",
        default="ws://localhost:8080",
        help="Relay URL (default: ws://localhost:8080)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--secret",
        help="Keypair secret hex (default: generate random)",
    )

    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Command to run")

    # publish command
    publish_parser = subparsers.add_parser("publish", help="Publish a template version")
    publish_parser.add_argument("template_dir", help="Path to template directory")
    publish_parser.add_argument("--summary", help="Summary of changes")

    # list command
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List templates from an author")
    list_parser.add_argument("author", help="Author pubkey")

    # show command
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show version history for a template")
    show_parser.add_argument("author", help="Author pubkey")
    show_parser.add_argument("name", help="Template name")

    return parser


async def _cmd_publish(args) -> int:
    """Execute publish command."""
    try:
        template_dir = Path(args.template_dir).resolve()
        if not template_dir.is_dir():
            print(f"error: template directory not found: {template_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
            return 1

        # Get keypair
        if args.secret:
            keypair = Keypair.from_hex(args.secret)
        else:
            keypair = Keypair.generate()
            print(f"Generated keypair: {keypair.public_key_hex}", file=sys.stderr)

        # Create publisher
        repo_root = template_dir.parent.parent.parent
        publisher = TemplatePublisher(repo_root, keypair)

        # Extract template info
        template_info = publisher.extract_template_info(template_dir)
        print(f"Publishing {template_info.name} v{template_info.version}")

        # Build events
        version_event = publisher.build_version_event(
            template_info, summary=args.summary or ""
        )
        pointer_event = publisher.build_pointer_event(version_event)

        # Sign events
        signed_version = keypair.sign_event(version_event)
        signed_pointer = keypair.sign_event(pointer_event)

        # Publish to relay
        config = CoreConfig(relay_url=args.relay)
        async with RelayClient(args.relay, keypair, config) as client:
            await client.publish(signed_version)
            print(f"Published version event: {signed_version.id}")

            await client.publish(signed_pointer)
            print(f"Published pointer event: {signed_pointer.id}")

        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1


async def _cmd_list(args) -> int:
    """Execute list command."""
    try:
        keypair = Keypair.generate()
        config = CoreConfig(relay_url=args.relay)

        async with RelayClient(args.relay, keypair, config) as client:
            reader = TemplateReader(client)

            # Query for all kind 42050 events by this author
            filters = [{"kinds": [42050], "authors": [args.author]}]
            events = await client.query(filters)

            # Group by template name
            templates: dict[str, list] = {}
            for event in events:
                name = event.first_tag_value("template_name")
                if name:
                    if name not in templates:
                        templates[name] = []
                    templates[name].append(event)

            if not templates:
                print("No templates found for this author")
                return 0

            for name in sorted(templates.keys()):
                versions = templates[name]
                print(f"{name}:")
                for event in sorted(versions, key=lambda e: e.first_tag_value("version") or ""):
                    version = event.first_tag_value("version")
                    git_ref = event.first_tag_value("git_ref")
                    print(f"  v{version} @ {git_ref}")

        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1


async def _cmd_show(args) -> int:
    """Execute show command."""
    try:
        keypair = Keypair.generate()
        config = CoreConfig(relay_url=args.relay)

        async with RelayClient(args.relay, keypair, config) as client:
            reader = TemplateReader(client)
            versions = await reader.get_version_history(args.author, args.name)

            if not versions:
                print(f"No versions found for {args.name}")
                return 0

            print(f"Template: {args.name}")
            print(f"Author: {args.author}")
            print(f"\nVersion history ({len(versions)} total):")
            for v in versions:
                print(f"  v{v.version} @ {v.git_ref}")
                print(f"    Event: {v.event_id}")
                print(f"    Date:  {v.created_at} ({v.created_at})")
                if v.summary:
                    print(f"    Summary: {v.summary}")
                if v.parent_version_id:
                    print(f"    Parent: {v.parent_version_id}")

        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1


async def main_async(args) -> int:
    """Execute the appropriate command."""
    if not args.command:
        return 1

    if args.command == "publish":
        return await _cmd_publish(args)
    elif args.command == "list":
        return await _cmd_list(args)
    elif args.command == "show":
        return await _cmd_show(args)
    else:
        print(f"unknown command: {args.command}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1


def main() -> None:
    """Entry point."""
    parser = _create_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.command:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(0)

    exit_code = asyncio.run(main_async(args))
    sys.exit(exit_code)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

=== setup.sh ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Environment setup -- runs at the start of every iteration
# ---------------------------------------------------------
#
# Must be idempotent. Update this script instead of installing
# packages inline, so the environment stays reproducible across
# iterations. The loop activates the repo venv automatically.
=== lint.sh ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run linters on the node's worktree
# ----------------------------------

# NODE_DIR walks up from this hook's seeded location -- the inverse of
# Node.node_dir's <worktree>[/<project>]/.fractal/<branch> derivation
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd -P)"
NODE_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
WORKTREE_DIR="$(git -C "$(dirname "$NODE_DIR")" rev-parse --show-toplevel)"

SKILLS_DIR="$NODE_DIR/skills"
MEMORY_DIR="$NODE_DIR/memory"

PROJECT="$(fractal config _get project --path="$WORKTREE_DIR" 2>/dev/null || echo ".")"
if [[ "$PROJECT" == "." ]]; then
    WIKI_DIR="$WORKTREE_DIR/wiki"
else
    WIKI_DIR="$WORKTREE_DIR/$PROJECT/wiki"
fi

if [[ -d "$SKILLS_DIR" ]]; then
    for ENTRY in "$SKILLS_DIR"/*; do
        [[ -e "$ENTRY" ]] || continue
        if [[ ! -d "$ENTRY" ]]; then
            echo "Error: skills/$(basename "$ENTRY") is not a directory" >&2
            exit 1
        fi
        if [[ ! -f "$ENTRY/SKILL.md" ]]; then
            echo "Error: skills/$(basename "$ENTRY")/ is missing SKILL.md" >&2
            exit 1
        fi
    done
fi

if command -v wiki &>/dev/null; then
    if [[ -f "$MEMORY_DIR/_index.md" ]]; then
        wiki lint --path="$MEMORY_DIR" \
            || echo "Warning: memory wiki ($MEMORY_DIR) has lint issues" >&2
    fi
    if [[ -f "$WIKI_DIR/_index.md" ]]; then
        wiki lint --path="$WIKI_DIR" \
            || echo "Warning: project wiki ($WIKI_DIR) has lint issues" >&2
    fi
fi
=== NODE.md ===
# NODE.md — evergreen

- **branch:** `main.evergreen`
- **parent:** `main` (root / user node)
- **scope:** `src`, `tests`
- **template:** `dev-node v2 @ c6696b7`
- **role:** first ply, product surface — the human's standing context
  environment over the signed log (DESIGN.md §5).

## Instructions

You are the **evergreen** node for Lindenmayer: you build the surface a human
governs their subgraph from. Your versioned contract is
`tree/evergreen/NODE.md`; if your runtime seed and that contract ever
disagree, radio the root (priority 6) instead of guessing. Design inputs, in
authority order: `docs/DESIGN.md` (**§5.1 is your charter** — read it before
anything else; then §5.2, §6 principles), then
`docs/research/evergreen/README.md` (the design aggregate), then core's and
registry's shipped APIs (`src/lindenmayer/core/`, `src/lindenmayer/registry/`
— build against them, never modify them).

**The ruling that defines your scope: evergreen v1 is the READ PLANE.**
Fractal's own CLI remains the write plane. You ship no radio-send UI, no
signal API, no live-edit UI, and no node-spawn bridge. Those are wrappers
around working Fractal capabilities; wrapping adds a second control path
with no new capability and drifts at §6.3. If a deliverable seems to need a
write path, that is a design question — radio the architect, do not build
it.

Deliverables, in `src/lindenmayer/evergreen/`:

1. **Read-only query library over the signed log.** One typed surface
   wrapping relay filters for 42010, 42020, 42030, 42040/42041, 42050,
   38150, and 42060 (compaction — read-side only; see the boundary note
   below). Reuse core's relay client; add no new client. **No local index**
   — reconstruct on demand, extending relay-as-cursor (bridge) and
   relay-as-registry (registry) to **relay-as-context** (§6.2). Verify from
   the signed log per §6.5: signatures and author attestation checked via
   core's verification module, never taken on the relay's word.
   Acceptance: query tests against a mock relay (reuse the existing
   in-process infra in `tests/`), including a tampered-event rejection case.

2. **Context-surface generator.** Emits the `CONTEXT.md`-shaped standing
   surface as a composite: a human-authored preamble (mission, phase,
   non-negotiables, governance mode, pointers) read from config, plus a
   generated situational block derived from deliverable 1 — live subgraph
   state, budgets and spend, pending approval gates, recent lifecycle.
   **Hard requirement: any display of the model policy READS the live
   assignment (node configs / step pins); it never restates the tiers.**
   Two independent surfaces restating that parameter have already gone
   stale, which is why §3 forbids it. Acceptance: golden test (fixture
   event set → expected surface), plus a test proving the model-policy
   block reflects a changed live assignment.

3. **History query CLI.** `lindenmayer-evergreen` — the "query own history"
   capability in full, the one §5 capability v1 owns end to end: a node's
   runs over time, cost/token rollups from 42020 (label every dollar figure
   **shadow cost**, §6), approval traces from 42040/42041, and
   template-version → instance linkage from 42050 + 42010. Stdlib
   `argparse`; a new runtime dependency is automatically an architect
   consultation. **The CLI argument path itself must be under test** — an
   E2E that invokes the module entry point with real arguments, not just
   the objects behind it. Both prior ply nodes shipped CLIs whose argument
   path no test ever executed, and both were broken on first live use.
   Acceptance: CLI-invocation E2E against a mock relay for each subcommand.

**Explicitly out of v1** (named so you do not drift into them): any write
path; a wire-visible decision-log kind (governance-shaped, adjacent to the
evals fence — the decision log stays a section of DESIGN.md); consent-grant
queries (§4 mechanics remain open in §8). Anything eval-shaped is an
automatic architect consultation.

**Boundary note:** kind 42060 compaction events are the **bridge's** to
emit, from its transcript adapter. You consume them read-side only. Do not
build emission, and do not wait on it — query what exists.

### Decomposition doctrine

Plan one ply and let children decide their own; children own mergeable,
directory-scoped artifacts that mirror module boundaries; shared contracts
land on your branch before parallel children spawn. Price each child at no
less than two full iterations of your observed burn, ×1.3. Cap-sizing
history is from cheaper-tier runs and is being restated against sonnet
figures — report your observed per-iteration burn in your outbox so the
architect can recalibrate §7.

### Model policy (tree standard)

You and your children run **sonnet**; every REVIEW step is pinned to
**opus** via step frontmatter (`model: opus`). The pin on a child is applied
by YOU, the spawning parent, before starting it — a child cannot edit its
own immutable seed — then verified after spawn. Work orders to children are
numbered, one decision per item, acceptance evidence named.

### Architect consultation covenant

`main.platform_architect` owns design coherence. STOP the affected work path
and radio its inbox (priority 6, with evidence) before proceeding on: a
change to a key component (event-kind semantics, an integration boundary,
another node's interface, anything DESIGN.md records as decided); a
deviation from this contract or a DESIGN.md principle, including ANY new
storage (§6.2 — the relay is the context store; no local index, no cache
files) or weakening of verification (§6.5); any write path; anything
eval-shaped; a new runtime dependency. Rejection is a veto; other work
continues while you wait; cite the verdict message id in the landing commit.
Consult on architecture, not on style.

### Standing constraints

- Never patch or fork Fractal or Buzz. Never write outside `src`, `tests` —
  `docs/` belongs to the architect; if your work needs a doc, radio it.
- Privacy is a wire-format property (§6.1): surface aggregates, never
  subgraph detail the log does not already carry. Never read compaction
  summary bodies — 42060 carries a hash for exactly that reason.
- All dollar figures are shadow cost; on instant zero-cost invocation
  failures (rate exhaustion), post priority 7 and finish with a handoff
  note rather than burning iterations.

## Completion Requirements

- All three deliverables exist and `bash $NODE_DIR/scripts/test.sh` passes
  the full suite: query tests with tampered-event rejection, the
  context-surface golden plus the live-model-policy test, and a CLI
  *invocation* E2E for every subcommand.
- The generated context surface is demonstrated against the live tree: run
  the generator over this repo's own relay data and commit the output as a
  sample under `tests/` fixtures (§7 dogfooding — the surface must be shown
  working on the tree that built it).
- Required escalations are SENT (never gated on replies).
- Observed per-iteration burn posted to your outbox for §7 recalibration.
- Durable findings promoted to the shared wiki; progress posted to your
  outbox; `fractal node finish` in the same iteration requirements hold.

aiTitle: Build Lindenmayer evergreen query library and CLI

content.content:
1	"""Deployment configuration: relay URL, key material, capability attestations.
2	
3	No new storage systems (DESIGN.md §6.2): configuration is a TOML file read
4	with stdlib ``tomllib`` plus environment variables. Key material is never
5	stored in the config file itself — the file names *where* the key lives
6	(an env var or a file path), and ``load_keypair`` resolves it at runtime.
7	
8	Capability attestations are the "verify, don't infer" mechanism from the
9	relay-integration research: relay-side behaviors that degrade silently on a
10	plain NIP-29 relay (private-group read gating above all) must be explicitly
11	attested by the deployment operator before the library will rely on them.
12	Absence of an attestation is a hard "no", not a "probably".
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import os
18	import tomllib
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
22	
23	from lindenmayer.core.keys import Keypair
24	
25	__all__ = [
26	    "CAP_PRIVATE_READ_GATING",
27	    "CapabilityAttestation",
28	    "ConfigError",
29	    "CoreConfig",
30	]
31	
32	# The one degradation that is not cosmetic (research aggregate, finding 4):
33	# relay-side enforcement of NIP-29 `private` read gating. Buzz satisfies it
34	# by construction; "NIP-29 compliant" alone does not.
35	CAP_PRIVATE_READ_GATING = "nip29-private-read-gating"
36	
37	
38	class ConfigError(ValueError):
39	    """Raised when configuration is missing, malformed, or unresolvable."""
40	
41	
42	class CapabilityAttestation(BaseModel):
43	    """An operator's explicit statement that this deployment's relay enforces
44	    a capability that the protocol alone does not guarantee.
45	
46	    This is a config-level record of an operational fact, attributable to
47	    whoever operates the deployment — not a signed wire event. Consumers
48	    that need wire-level proof verify from the signed log (verify module).
49	    """
50	
51	    capability: str
52	    attested_by: str = Field(description="operator identity: name or pubkey hex")
53	    attested_at: int = Field(description="unix timestamp of the attestation")
54	    note: str = ""
55	
56	    @field_validator("capability", "attested_by")
57	    @classmethod
58	    def _non_empty(cls, value: str) -> str:
59	        if not value.strip():
60	            raise ValueError("must be non-empty")
61	        return value
62	
63	
64	class CoreConfig(BaseModel):
65	    """Top-level configuration for a Lindenmayer deployment."""
66	
67	    relay_url: str
68	    capability_attestations: list[CapabilityAttestation] = Field(default_factory=list)
69	    secret_key_env: str = "LINDENMAYER_SECRET_KEY"
70	    secret_key_file: Path | None = None
71	
72	    @field_validator("relay_url")
73	    @classmethod
74	    def _ws_url(cls, value: str) -> str:
75	        if not value.startswith(("ws://", "wss://")):
76	            raise ValueError("relay_url must be a ws:// or wss:// URL")
77	        return value
78	
79	    # -- capabilities ------------------------------------------------------
80	
81	    def has_capability(self, capability: str) -> bool:
82	        """True only if the deployment explicitly attests ``capability``."""
83	        return any(a.capability == capability for a in self.capability_attestations)
84	
85	    def attestation_for(self, capability: str) -> CapabilityAttestation | None:
86	        for attestation in self.capability_attestations:
87	            if attestation.capability == capability:
88	                return attestation
89	        return None
90	
91	    # -- key material ------------------------------------------------------
92	
93	    def load_keypair(self) -> Keypair:
94	        """Resolve key material: env var first, then key file.
95	
96	        The secret is 64-char hex. It never transits the config file; a
97	        world-readable key file is refused outright.
98	        """
99	        secret_hex = os.environ.get(self.secret_key_env, "").strip()
100	        if secret_hex:
101	            return self._keypair_from_hex(secret_hex, source=f"${self.secret_key_env}")
102	        if self.secret_key_file is not None:
103	            path = self.secret_key_file.expanduser()
104	            if not path.is_file():
105	                raise ConfigError(f"secret key file not found: {path}")
106	            mode = path.stat().st_mode & 0o777
107	            if mode & 0o077:
108	                raise ConfigError(
109	                    f"secret key file {path} is group/world-accessible "
110	                    f"(mode {mode:o}); chmod 600 it"
111	                )
112	            return self._keypair_from_hex(path.read_text().strip(), source=str(path))
113	        raise ConfigError(
114	            f"no key material: ${self.secret_key_env} is unset and no "
115	            "secret_key_file is configured"
116	        )
117	
118	    @staticmethod
119	    def _keypair_from_hex(secret_hex: str, *, source: str) -> Keypair:
120	        try:
121	            return Keypair.from_hex(secret_hex)
122	        except ValueError as exc:
123	            # Deliberately excludes the material itself from the message.
124	            raise ConfigError(f"invalid secret key from {source}: {exc}") from exc
125	
126	    # -- loading -----------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	    @classmethod
129	    def from_toml(cls, path: str | Path) -> "CoreConfig":
130	        path = Path(path)
131	        try:
132	            data = tomllib.loads(path.read_text())
133	        except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError) as exc:
134	            raise ConfigError(f"cannot read config {path}: {exc}") from exc
135	        try:
136	            return cls.model_validate(data)
137	        except ValueError as exc:
138	            raise ConfigError(f"invalid config {path}: {exc}") from exc
139
